mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.3% as of June 2023 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,380 thousand as of June 2023
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon falls below 0.02 in any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and forward-looking economic deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 495,381 million and HOUST at 1,297 thousand as of May 2023
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflation risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates and lower growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points month-over-month while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% and CPIAUCSL at 247.963 as of June 2023
regime_hint: stagflation or monetary policy tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between UNRATE and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis directly reflects aggregate demand strength; sustained positive growth signals healthy economic fundamentals that support corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,163.894 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2023
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than negative growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which dampens aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices adjust to lower earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.2 percentage points or more month-over-month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 20-day horizon falls below 0.02 in any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction activity and residential investment; a sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening forward demand and reduced multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate demand-side weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,297 thousand units as of May 2023
regime_hint: housing market downturn or demand-side slowdown
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following sharp HOUST declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
